stories.title: Truist Championship Final Round — Fitzpatrick Leads, Young Lurking, McIlroy Implodes

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
FINAL ROUND TODAY at Quail Hollow — $20M Signature Event.

Alex Fitzpatrick leads at 14-under.
Kristoffer Reitan 13-under.
Cameron Young fired a 63 — now 12-under, two back.

Rory McIlroy? Shot 75 in R3. Out of it. #TruistChampionship #PGATour

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Cameron Young already won the Players and the Cadillac Championship this year.

Now he's 2 back at Quail Hollow with 18 holes left.

Can he make it three wins? #TruistChampionship #PGATour

facebook.long_form:
The final round of the 2026 Truist Championship is underway at Quail Hollow Club in Charlotte — and the leaderboard heading into Sunday is about as exciting as it gets.

Alex Fitzpatrick enters the day as the 54-hole leader at 14-under par after firing a 7-under 64 in Saturday's round. Sitting one shot behind him at 13-under is Norway's Kristoffer Reitan, who also shot 64 on Saturday. And lurking just two back at 12-under is Cameron Young — who came in hot with a 63 and knows exactly how to close on a Sunday. Sungjae Im, who led through 36 holes, sits at 11-under and is three back.

The story nobody wanted but everybody expected: Rory McIlroy completely unraveled in Round 3. The Northern Irishman had been four shots back and in the mix entering Saturday but shot a 4-over 75 — six bogeys against only two birdies — to effectively end his bid for a record fifth Truist title.

At stake today: $20M and 700 FedExCup points. And for Cameron Young, potentially a third PGA Tour win in the same spring.
facebook.image_caption: Fitzpatrick leads. Young is lurking with a 63. McIlroy shot 75 and is out. Final round at Quail Hollow today. #TruistChampionship #PGATour

article.headline: Truist Championship Final Round: Fitzpatrick Leads as Young, Reitan Give Chase and McIlroy Exits

stories.title: LIV Virginia Final Round — Herbert 3 Clear, Ballester's Stunning 60 Creates Drama

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Final round of LIV Virginia is TODAY.

Lucas Herbert leads at 21-under (-3 ahead of Garcia).

Josele Ballester shot a BOGEY-FREE 60 in R3 and is 13-under.

This is going to be wild. #LIVGolf #LIVVirginia

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Bryson DeChambeau and Jon Rahm are both within 9 shots of Lucas Herbert at LIV Virginia.

Garcia is at 18-under, 3 back.

And someone shot a 60 yesterday.

LIV Sunday golf is on right now. #LIVGolf

facebook.long_form:
Sunday golf at LIV Virginia promises fireworks — and Lucas Herbert better have his game face ready.

The Australian leads the Maaden LIV Golf Virginia tournament at Trump National Golf Club in Sterling, Virginia at 21-under par, three shots ahead of Sergio Garcia (18-under) heading into the final round. But the story of Saturday was Josele Ballester, who fired an absolutely stunning bogey-free 60 in Round 3 — a 12-under round — to move into the group at 13-under. He shares that position with Scott Vincent, Bryson DeChambeau, and Dean Burmester. Jon Rahm lurks at 12-under.

For Herbert, a first LIV individual title would be a significant career milestone. He has been dominant through three rounds — through 36 holes he set a LIV 36-hole Strokes Gained record — but a three-shot lead is not safe when Garcia is the closest challenger and someone is firing 60s. This $30M event could produce one of the most dramatic finishes of the LIV season.
facebook.image_caption: Herbert leads at 21-under. Garcia 3 back. Ballester shot a BOGEY-FREE 60 to get within 8. Final round TODAY at LIV Virginia. #LIVGolf

article.headline: LIV Virginia Final Round: Herbert Leads at 21-Under as Ballester's Stunning 60 Tightens the Field

stories.title: PGA Championship Preview — Aronimink, Spieth's Grand Slam Quest, and the World's Best Field

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
4 days until the PGA Championship.

Scheffler defends. McIlroy chases major No. 7. Jordan Spieth hunts the career Grand Slam.

Aronimink Golf Club. May 14-17. ESPN/CBS.

Get ready. #PGAChampionship #Aronimink

facebook.long_form:
One of the most anticipated second majors in recent memory is just four days away — and the storylines at Aronimink Golf Club are stacking up.

The 108th PGA Championship runs May 14-17 at Aronimink in Newtown Square, Pennsylvania. It's the first PGA Championship held at this historic Donald Ross design since 1962, when Gary Player lifted the Wanamaker Trophy. The course will demand precision and patience — and the world's best players will be tested from day one.

Here are the four biggest storylines heading in:

**Scheffler defends.** The World No. 1 and 2025 PGA champion skipped the Truist Championship to prepare for Aronimink. He's been runner-up three times running in recent weeks — the competitive hunger is clearly there.

**McIlroy's career at the crossroads.** Rory has won this event twice (2012, 2014) and a third would take his career major total to seven — putting him in truly elite company. After his R3 75 at Truist, he'll arrive at Aronimink with plenty of motivation.

**Spieth's Grand Slam quest.** Jordan Spieth has everything — Masters, US Open, Open Championship — except the PGA. This is his 10th attempt at completing the career Grand Slam. No one in history has won their final Grand Slam leg at the PGA Championship, but Spieth doesn't care about that stat.

**Cameron Young's triple threat.** Young has already won the Players and the Cadillac Championship. A PGA title would make him one of the most decorated single-season performers in modern golf.

TV: ESPN handles Thursday-Friday, CBS takes Saturday-Sunday. Tee times begin May 14.
facebook.image_caption: Scheffler defends. McIlroy chases No. 7. Spieth hunts the Grand Slam. The PGA Championship begins May 14 at Aronimink. #PGAChampionship

article.headline: PGA Championship Preview: Aronimink Returns to Major Golf With Scheffler, McIlroy, Spieth's Grand Slam, and Young's Historic Run

stories.title: Korda Hall of Fame Countdown — Four Points Away, Kroger Queen City Next

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda: 23 of 27 LPGA Hall of Fame points.

4 away from automatic qualification.

She tees it up next week at the Kroger Queen City Championship in Cincinnati (May 14-17).

Can she close it out? #LPGA #NellyKorda

facebook.long_form:
Nelly Korda is four points from golfing immortality — and her next chance to add them comes in just four days.

The World No. 1 sits at 23 of the 27 LPGA Hall of Fame points required for automatic qualification heading into the 2026 Kroger Queen City Championship at Maketewah Country Club in Cincinnati, Ohio (May 14-17). An event win earns two Hall of Fame points. Win twice in the coming weeks and Korda earns her spot in the Hall of Fame on her own terms, as the most dominant active player in the women's game.

The context is remarkable. Korda already has three LPGA victories in 2026 — including the Chevron Championship major — and has not finished outside the top 5 in any of her 2026 starts. Seven of the world's top 10 are entered in Cincinnati for what should be a stacked field.

Korda is already a generational talent. The Hall of Fame would make it official. Cincinnati could be where it happens.
facebook.image_caption: 23 of 27 LPGA Hall of Fame points. Just 4 away from automatic qualification. Nelly Korda goes to Cincinnati next week. Can she close it out? #LPGA #NellyKorda

article.headline: Korda Is Four Points From the LPGA Hall of Fame — Can She Seal It in Cincinnati?

stories.title: McIlroy's Truist Implosion — R3 75 Ends Quail Hollow Record Bid

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Rory McIlroy was 4 shots back entering Round 3.

Then he shot a 75. Six bogeys. Two birdies.

Out of contention at Quail Hollow. A record 5th title will have to wait.

Next target: PGA Championship, May 14. #RoryMcIlroy #PGATour

facebook.long_form:
Twenty-four hours can change everything in golf — and rarely in a worse way than what Rory McIlroy experienced Saturday at Quail Hollow.

McIlroy entered Round 3 of the 2026 Truist Championship four shots off the lead after a solid 67 in Round 2. He had the course history — four wins at Quail Hollow — and the motivation to chase a record fifth title. Then came Saturday's round: a 4-over 75, featuring six bogeys and only two birdies, that erased him from contention entirely.

It's the kind of round that happens to every professional eventually — but the timing stings. McIlroy was building momentum toward a major week and instead takes a confidence blow heading into the PGA Championship at Aronimink (May 14-17). He won this tournament in 2010, 2015, 2021, and 2024. The record fifth title will have to wait until 2027 at the earliest.

The silver lining: there's no better way to reset than chasing a major championship. McIlroy won the PGA twice (2012, 2014) and knows Aronimink presents a genuine opportunity for redemption.
facebook.image_caption: Four shots back entering R3. Then a 75 with six bogeys. McIlroy's Quail Hollow record bid is over. Next stop: Aronimink and the PGA Championship, May 14. #RoryMcIlroy

article.headline: McIlroy's 75 at Quail Hollow Ends Truist Title Bid — Now He Heads to Aronimink With Something to Prove
